repositories.brief_summary: String module enhancements, GC tweaks mechanism, type-checker determinism fixes, and FreeBSD profiling support merged
repositories.new_features_summary: String split/replace functions, Gc.Tweak module for runtime parameters, FreeBSD frame pointer support, and deterministic compilation
repositories.new_features:
- **String module enhancements** with `split_first`, `split_last`, `split_all`, and `rsplit_all` functions for splitting strings by separator ([#14437](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14437)) plus `replace_first`, `replace_last`, and `replace_all` for substring replacement ([#14436](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14436)) by [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli)
- **Gc.Tweak module** providing named GC parameters accessible via `Gc.Tweak.set "foo" 42` or `OCAMLRUNPARAM='Xfoo=42'` for GC experimentation and tuning ([#14367](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14367)) by [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan) and Nick Barnes
- **FreeBSD frame pointer support** for AMD64 and ARM64 platforms enabling profiling with `pmcstat` and `dtrace`, with documentation in the manual ([#14486](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14486)) by [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist)
- **Deterministic compilation** restored by resetting type ID and level counters between compilations, making `ocamlc -c foo.mli foo.ml` produce identical output to separate invocations ([#14483](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14483)) by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27)
- **Windows minimum version** now explicitly set to Windows 8/Server 2012 via `_WIN32_WINNT` macro defined consistently across codebase ([#14484](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14484)) by [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA), fixing [#7418](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/7418)
- **AI contribution guidelines** added in AI.md and CONTRIBUTING.md requiring disclosure of substantial AI authorship ([#14052](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14052), [#14383](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14383)) by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) and [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell)
repositories.activity_summary: Six new PRs opened including polymorphic pattern fixes and CFI support, with ongoing discussions about stdlib unstable features
repositories.activity:
- **Six new PRs opened** this week including soundness fix for polymorphic parameters by [Alistair](https://github.com/johnyob) ([#14480](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14480)) addressing [#14434](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14434), alternative fix by [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan) ([#14479](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14479)), Power backend CFI support by [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist) ([#14482](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14482)), ocp-indent formatting configuration by [Alistair](https://github.com/johnyob) ([#14485](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14485)), and proposed `String.drop_prefix` additions by [Michael Neumann](https://github.com/mneumann) ([#14487](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14487))
- **Runtime and compiler improvements** delivered through opcode synchronization simplification eliminating `jumptbl.h` and `opnames.h` using X-Macro pattern ([#14488](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14488)), runtime header include fixes allowing independent inclusion ([#14489](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14489)), and C++ compatibility improvements suppressing flexible array member warnings ([#14490](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14490)) by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27)
- **Shape occurrences bug fix** resolving assertion failure in `env.ml` by extending `Env.find_uid` to handle extended paths for constructors and labels ([#14476](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14476)) by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron), fixing [#14474](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14474)
- **Internal refactoring** using C2y `_Countof` operator for array size calculations with type safety ([#14413](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14413)) and labeling boolean argument in `typing/env.ml` for readability ([#14477](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14477)) by [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) and [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche)
- **Opam package management** updated with missing conflict declaration for compiler packages ([#14475](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14475)) by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate)
- **PRs closed without merge** including unstable attribute proposal ([#14430](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14430)) after developer meeting showed no consensus on ecosystem implications, automated stdlib labeling workflow ([#14467](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14467)) replaced by giving stdlib team triaging rights, and constructor redefinition warning ([#12312](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/12312)) closed per consensus in [#10655](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/10655)
- **Ongoing review activity** on mutex-protected lazy thunks proposal ([#14393](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14393)) by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche), `Fun.todo` addition approved by stdlib team ([#13101](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13101)) by [Pavlo Khrystenko](https://github.com/pkhry), continuation representation optimization ([#14469](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14469)) by [KC Sivaramakrishnan](https://github.com/kayceesrk), labeled tuple projections ([#14257](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14257)) by [Alistair](https://github.com/johnyob), and arm64 miscompilation fix ([#14010](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14010)) by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell)
repositories.discussions_summary: String module improvements and non-empty list type proposals under consideration
repositories.notable_discussions:
- **String module enhancement proposal** by [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli) presenting comprehensive API additions including index search with predicates, substring searching/replacing, and string breaking operations using consistent `first/last/all` naming convention ([#14137](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14137))
- **Non-empty list type** proposal by [Mathieu Barbin](https://github.com/mbarbin) suggesting adding type definition without API to stdlib for third-party library compatibility: `type 'a t = ( :: ) of 'a * 'a list` ([#14261](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14261))
- **Signal handler SA_RESTART flag** discussion initiated by [Christophe Raffalli](https://github.com/craff) proposing default use of SA_RESTART to reduce EINTR handling complexity ([#14481](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14481))
- **Unstable features mechanism** debated in developer meeting regarding [#14430](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14430) with concerns about ecosystem fragmentation and maintenance burden leading to no consensus
- **Domain.DLS documentation** improvements requested across multiple issues ([#11905](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/11905), [#11920](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/11920), [#11921](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/11921)) highlighting need for better examples and performance guidance
repositories.trends_summary: Focus on stdlib enhancements, Windows platform support, profiling infrastructure, and deterministic compilation

- **AI contribution policies** established with clear disclosure requirements and contributor responsibility guidelines ([#14052](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14052), [#14383](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14383))

repositories.discussions_summary: Windows installation SSL connectivity issue and 0install solver resolving multiple mingw architectures unexpectedly
repositories.notable_discussions:
- **Windows installation failure** reported by [@WellDoneYouWin](https://github.com/WellDoneYouWin) with curl SSL handshake error (exit code 35) preventing repository index download during opam initialization on Windows with Cygwin ([#6847](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6847)); [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli) diagnosed as network connectivity or outdated system certificates issue
- **0install solver behavior** with mingw packages discussed by [@tobil4sk](https://github.com/tobil4sk), where builtin-0install resolver installs both i686 and x86_64 mingw architecture variants simultaneously for conf-zlib and conf-libpcre2-8, unlike builtin-mccs+glpk which installs only x86_64 variants ([#6843](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6843)); issue appears related to dependency constraints in opam-repository packages

repositories.brief_summary: Three merlin library improvements focused on locate-types enhancements and record field completion fix
repositories.new_features_summary: Enhanced locate-types with 'Other' variant support and optional parameter handling, plus record field autocompletion fix
repositories.new_features:
- **locate-types command** now includes an `Other` variant in results, enabling editors to display type variables and other non-navigable types more accurately when reconstructing original type structures ([#2025](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2025))
- **Optional parameter handling** improved in locate-types by stripping the `option` wrapper from optional parameters, so `?foo:int` now shows `int` instead of `int option` ([#2027](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2027))
- **Record field autocompletion** fixed by adding `for_completion` field to reader cache fingerprint, resolving issue where completion after a dot produced no field candidates ([#2028](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2028))
repositories.activity_summary: One new PR proposes enclosing command enhancements for region-based queries, while ongoing work includes test additions for destruct and type-enclosing behaviors
repositories.activity:
- **New `-stop` flag proposed** for the `enclosing` command by [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) to enable region-based queries instead of point-based, particularly useful for `locate-types` and `annotate-type` commands when working with expression fragments ([#2029](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2029))
- **OCaml 5.5 support tracking** opened by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) to monitor progress as OCaml 5.5 approaches its freeze, noting that several large PRs need to merge before the first alpha release ([#2024](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2024))
- **Test additions ongoing** for existing issues including [Tim ats](https://github.com/Tim-ats-d)'s reproduction test for destruct command behavior ([#2002](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2002), [#1786](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1786)) and type-enclosing inconsistencies ([#2004](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2004), [#1949](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1949))
repositories.discussions_summary: Design discussion around region-based queries for LSP protocol limitations in OCaml's expression-heavy structure
repositories.notable_discussions:
- **Region-based query support** discussed in [#2029](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2029) where [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) and [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) explored how to handle expression selections in LSP, with [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) suggesting alternative naming (`-end-position`) and noting that similar functionality already exists in `type-enclosing` using the full results list
- **Record field completion bug** investigation in [#2026](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2026) identified cache fingerprinting as the root cause, with [Kostia Balytskyi](https://github.com/ikostia) providing detailed analysis of reader cache behavior and [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) offering code review suggestions for the fix implementation
repositories.trends_summary: Continued focus on improving type navigation and editor integration through locate-types enhancements and LSP protocol adaptations
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Type navigation improvements** continue with multiple enhancements to the `locate-types` command, building on previous work to provide better editor integration for jump-to-definition and type inspection features ([#2025](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2025), [#2027](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2027))
- **LSP protocol adaptation** efforts addressing OCaml's expression-heavy structure, with proposals to extend commands like `enclosing` to better handle region selections rather than just point positions ([#2029](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2029))
- **Test coverage expansion** for edge cases and reported issues, ensuring proper regression testing for behaviors like destruct command ([#2002](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2002)) and type-enclosing inconsistencies ([#2004](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2004))
repositories.contributors_summary: Three contributors merged improvements while maintainers provided extensive code review and design feedback
repositories.contributors:
- [Kostia Balytskyi](https://github.com/ikostia) identified and fixed the record field completion bug ([#2028](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2028)) with comprehensive test coverage and addressed multiple review comments from [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw)
- [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) implemented two locate-types enhancements: adding the `Other` variant for better type reconstruction ([#2025](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2025)) and stripping option wrappers from optional parameters ([#2027](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2027)), both with changelog updates after review feedback
- [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) provided extensive code reviews across all merged PRs, proposed the `-stop` flag enhancement for the enclosing command ([#2029](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2029)), and engaged in design discussions about region-based query support
- [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) reviewed PRs and provided architectural feedback on the enclosing command proposal, suggesting alternative naming and noting existing similar functionality in `type-enclosing` ([#2029](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2029))
- [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) opened the OCaml 5.5 support tracking issue to coordinate upcoming API changes ([#2024](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2024))
- [Tim ats](https://github.com/Tim-ats-d) contributed test cases for existing issues related to destruct command and type-enclosing behaviors ([#2002](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2002), [#2004](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2004))

repositories.brief_summary: Git repository locking feature merged, critical select field bug fixed, extensive refactoring by Rudi Grinberg
repositories.new_features_summary: Git repo commit hash locking, select field fix for MirageOS, file descriptor leak fix, RPC server behavior change
repositories.new_features:
- **Git repository locking** now records commit hashes when locking git repositories, improving build reproducibility and avoiding unnecessary git pulls ([#13297](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13297)) by [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV)
- Fixed **select field evaluation** bug that was breaking all MirageOS unikernels after lwt 6.0.0, properly evaluating closure when resolving select fields with unavailable optional dependencies ([#13389](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13389), [#13299](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13299)) by [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro)
- Fixed **file descriptor leak** causing "too many open files" errors by adding missing `O_CLOEXEC` to redirect files ([#13385](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13385), [#13379](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13379)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- **Option to skip test dependencies** when building packages, avoiding unnecessary test dependency compilation ([#13298](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13298), [#12658](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/12658)) by [Sudha Parimala](https://github.com/Sudha247)
- **Detection of invalid git repositories** to prevent permanent bricking when git-repo folder is corrupt ([#13398](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13398)) by [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV)
- **Windows PATH handling improvements** using Env_path helper for correct PATH separator (: vs ;) ([#13405](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13405), [#12433](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/12433)) by [Puneeth Chaganti](https://github.com/punchagan)
- **Relocatable compiler support** for OCaml 5.5 by resolving symlinks from install actions, enabling better caching ([#13239](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13239), [#13229](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13229)) by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter)
- **RPC server no longer starts** with `dune promote` command ([#13428](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13428)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)

repositories.brief_summary: New RFC proposed for persistent compiler processes to reduce build times
repositories.activity_summary: New RFC for persistent compiler processes and ongoing discussion of labeled tuple projections
repositories.activity:
- [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) opened RFC [#60](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/pull/60) proposing **persistent compiler processes** to reduce compilation times by keeping compiler instances running across multiple invocations, avoiding repeated process startup costs and `.cmi` file loading
- The **persistent compiler RFC** generated discussion about implementation details including protocol design ([Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) suggested potential for merging Merlin capabilities), performance expectations ([Alain Frisch](https://github.com/alainfrisch) noted 10-20% gains are worthwhile despite not transforming incremental builds), and I/O handling approaches ([Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli) recommended streaming responses and file-based stdio redirection)
- Ongoing discussion on [Alistair](https://github.com/johnyob)'s **labeled tuple projections** RFC [#57](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/pull/57) with debate about tuple polymorphism, layout normalization, and the scope of the proposal
repositories.discussions_summary: Technical discussion on persistent compiler implementation and labeled tuple polymorphism trade-offs
repositories.notable_discussions:
- **Persistent compiler protocol design** debated between captured output approach (used by Bazel) versus file-based stdio redirection (used by Buck2), with concerns about temporary file overhead on Windows raised by [Alain Frisch](https://github.com/alainfrisch) ([#60](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/pull/60))
- **Performance expectations** for persistent mode discussed with [Alain Frisch](https://github.com/alainfrisch) arguing 10-20% compilation gains justify the relatively lightweight proposal, while [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli) noted gains primarily affect cold builds rather than cached incremental builds ([#60](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/pull/60))
- **Developer meeting consensus** on persistent compiler RFC indicates interest but requires caching `.cmi` loading experiments to demonstrate sufficient performance gains beyond the 10-20% baseline, with careful attention to reproducibility concerns around internal object IDs ([#60](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/pull/60))
- **Tuple polymorphism scope** clarified by [Alistair](https://github.com/johnyob) in response to concerns about typechecker complexity, implicit layout reordering breaking FFI guarantees, and the diminished motivation for polymorphic `fst`/`snd` after omnidirectional typing improvements ([#57](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/pull/57))
repositories.trends_summary: Focus on build system integration and compilation performance optimization
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Build system integration** emerging as central design consideration with references to Buck2 and Bazel worker protocols informing the persistent compiler RFC design ([#60](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/pull/60))
- **Performance versus complexity trade-offs** continuing to shape RFC discussions, with proposals requiring demonstration of concrete benefits sufficient to justify implementation and maintenance costs ([#60](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/pull/60), [#57](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/pull/57))
- **Type system evolution** discussions increasingly considering interaction with existing features and implementation complexity in the typechecker, particularly for structural polymorphism mechanisms ([#57](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/pull/57))
repositories.contributors_summary: Nicolás Ojeda Bär proposed persistent compiler RFC with review feedback from multiple core contributors
repositories.contributors:
- [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) authored the persistent compiler processes RFC [#60](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/pull/60) and engaged in detailed technical discussion about protocol design and implementation approaches
- [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) provided feedback on the persistent compiler RFC suggesting potential integration with Merlin capabilities ([#60](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/pull/60))
- [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli) contributed extensive review feedback on protocol design, build system interactions, and I/O handling approaches for the persistent compiler RFC ([#60](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/pull/60))
- [Alain Frisch](https://github.com/alainfrisch) participated in discussion about performance expectations and implementation concerns for the persistent compiler RFC ([#60](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/pull/60))
- [Alistair](https://github.com/johnyob) responded to feedback on the labeled tuple projections RFC [#57](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/pull/57) clarifying scope and addressing concerns about tuple polymorphism

repositories.brief_summary: Active week with 29 packages merged including new tools opam-repomin, csexp-query, ortools, domainpc, and updates to liquidsoap, menhir ecosystem
repositories.new_features_summary: New packages: opam-repomin for repository minimization, csexp-query for s-expression queries, ortools OCaml bindings, domainpc for multicore
repositories.new_features:
- **opam-repomin 1.0.0** by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm): Minimizes opam repository overlays into unified repos using opam-0install solver to determine minimum package sets ([#29274](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29274))
- **csexp-query 0.1.0** by [Josh Berdine](https://github.com/jberdine): Query tool for canonical s-expressions with field, index, each, pipe, cat, and this operations ([#29273](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29273))
- **ortools 9.14.0** by [Timothy Bourke](https://github.com/tbrk): OCaml bindings for Google OR-Tools models and CP-SAT solver, initial release with incomplete interface ([#29159](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29159))
- **domainpc 0.1** by [Hichem R. A.](https://github.com/hra687261): Spawns OCaml domains ensuring they run on separate cores for better multicore performance ([#29223](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29223))
- **shakuhachi 0.2.0** by [@EruEri](https://github.com/EruEri): Music collection manager with breaking changes to artist metadata format, plugin name fixes, improved metadata parsing, and license change from GPL-3.0 to MPL-2.0 ([#29275](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29275))
- **dead_code_analyzer 1.1.0** by [Corentin De Souza](https://github.com/fantazio): LexiFi's dead code analyzer for OCaml projects ([#29262](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29262))
- **qdrant 0.2.0** by [Jeong-Sik Yun](https://github.com/jeong-sik): OCaml client library for Qdrant vector database ([#29267](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29267))
- **restricted 1.2.0** by [@stopusingghpls](https://github.com/stopusingghpls): Linux filesystem sandboxing via landlock(7), added semantic versioning, tested on x86_64-openbsd and Linux with Landlock ABI v7 ([#29233](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29233))
- **raga 0.1.1** by [Kaustubh Maske Patil](https://github.com/nikochiko): Simple static site generator using Handlebars templating ([#29197](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29197))
- **camels 1.0.0** by [yomimono](https://github.com/yomimono): Game about camels, 2026 Community Edition using notty-community for cross-platform terminal support ([#29265](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29265))
- **slipshow 0.8.0** by [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd): Presentation tool with mirror mode for dual displays, external file support, and documentation rework ([#29249](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29249))
- **aslref 1.0~draft.2** by [Hadrien Renaud](https://github.com/HadrienRenaud): Arm Architecture Specification Language reference implementation ([#29246](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29246))
- **lmdb 1.1** by [@madroach](https://github.com/madroach): Fixed bad allocation pattern causing segfaults, deprecated old iterators, added Map.close and Map.to_dispenser iterator implementation ([#29212](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29212))
- **liquidsoap 2.4.2** by [Romain Beauxis](https://github.com/toots): Savonet audio streaming tool update covering liquidsoap, liquidsoap-js, liquidsoap-lang, and liquidsoap-mode packages ([#29235](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29235))
- **multicore-magic 2.3.2** by [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic): Low-level multicore utilities with unboxed Atomic_array support on OCaml 5.4 ([#29241](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29241))
- **print-table 0.1.3** by [Mathieu Barbin](https://github.com/mbarbin): Simple Unicode/ANSI and Markdown text table rendering library ([#29279](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29279))
repositories.activity_summary: Twenty new PRs opened including libbinaryen, menhir 20260122, capnp-rpc, patricia-tree; ecosystem updates for cmdliner 2.0+ and menhir compatibility
repositories.activity:
- **Newly opened PRs** this week include [Oscar Spencer](https://github.com/ospencer)'s **libbinaryen 125.0.0** bindings for Grain ([#29280](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29280)), [François Pottier](https://github.com/fpottier)'s **menhir 20260122** LR(1) parser generator release ([#29263](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29263)), [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm)'s **capnp-rpc 2.1.1** with cmdliner 2.0+ compatibility ([#29277](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29277)), [Dorian Lesbre](https://github.com/dlesbre)'s **patricia-tree 0.12.0** with mutex protection for multithreading ([#29247](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29247)), [Matthieu Lemerre](https://github.com/mlemerre)'s **codex 1.0~rc4** static analyzer ([#29259](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29259)), and [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube)'s **ezcurl 0.3** friendly OCurl wrapper ([#29257](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29257)) and **ambient-context 0.1.1** for thread-local storage ([#29248](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29248))
- **Robur projects** dominated releases with [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure) merging six packages: **flux 0.0.1~beta2** composable streaming abstractions with Miou ([#29252](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29252)), **carton 1.2.0** PACKv2 file implementation with reorganized distribution ([#29253](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29253)), **bstr 0.0.4** bigstring library with fixed C stubs ([#29254](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29254)), **cachet 0.0.4** mmap cache system for block devices ([#29250](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29250)), plus opening **rowex, bank, bancos 0.0.1** read-optimistic write-exclusive KV-store ([#29251](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29251))
- **Compiler and ecosystem updates** included [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron)'s **ocaml.5.5.0+trunk** update pointing to newly created 5.5 branch ([#29264](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29264)), [François Pottier](https://github.com/fpottier)'s ecosystem-wide constraint additions for **menhir 20260112** compatibility affecting archetype, bnfgen, otoml, catala, dolmen, morbig, touist, unionFind, and embedded_ocaml_templates ([#29222](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29222))
- **ReScript and Melange ecosystem** advanced with [David Sancho](https://github.com/davesnx) merging **styled-ppx 0.61.0** type-safe styled components with breaking ppxlib 0.36 update ([#29227](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29227)) and **reason 3.17.3** fixing stack overflow on Pconstraint ([#29258](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29258))
- **Windows compatibility** work continued with [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust) fixing **notty-community** build failure on Windows due to missing sys/ioctl.h ([#29268](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29268)), enabling [yomimono](https://github.com/yomimono)'s camels game release
- **Security and verification tools** expanded with [José Nogueira](https://github.com/thatportugueseguy)'s **passage 0.3.2** secret management removing devkit/re2 dependencies ([#29255](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29255)), while [Yu-Yang Lin](https://github.com/LaifsV1)'s **yultracer 0.2.0** bounded safety checker ([#29245](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29245)) and [Jonathan D.A. Jewell](https://github.com/hyperpolymath)'s **proven 0.9.0** formally verified primitives ([#29228](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29228)) await fixes
- **Presentation and documentation tools** improved with [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) releasing slipshow 0.8.0 then immediately fixing it with 0.8.1 ([#29249](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29249), [#29261](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29261))
- **Package management issues** surfaced with [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) proposing stricter **immutable policy** to restrict git URLs in pin-depends ([#29072](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29072)), and multiple PRs experiencing dependency resolution problems
- **Quality control concerns** emerged around [Jonathan D.A. Jewell](https://github.com/hyperpolymath)'s **proven** package appearing AI-generated with multiple lint errors and build failures without pre-submission testing ([#29228](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29228)), and [Михаил](https://github.com/dx3mod)'s **intel_hex 0.2** having bad checksum in tarball ([#29266](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29266))

- **CI and testing challenges** became apparent with [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube)'s ezcurl requiring network access incompatible with CI sandboxing ([#29257](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29257)), and [Matthieu Lemerre](https://github.com/mlemerre)'s codex having curl commands in build conflicting with CI ([#29259](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29259))
repositories.trends_summary: Ecosystem adapting to menhir and ppxlib updates, increasing multicore utilities, Windows compatibility focus, CI sandboxing vs real-world testing tensions
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Ecosystem constraint cascades** from core tools like menhir requiring coordinated updates across numerous packages, with [François Pottier](https://github.com/fpottier) adding `menhir < 20260112` bounds to archetype, bnfgen, otoml, catala, dolmen, morbig, touist, unionFind, and embedded_ocaml_templates ([#29222](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29222)), and new menhir release causing libsail compilation failures ([#29263](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29263))
- **Multicore OCaml ecosystem growth** with packages like [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic)'s multicore-magic 2.3.2 adding unboxed Atomic_array for OCaml 5.4 ([#29241](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29241)), [Hichem R. A.](https://github.com/hra687261)'s domainpc ensuring domains run on separate cores ([#29223](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29223)), and [Dorian Lesbre](https://github.com/dlesbre)'s patricia-tree adding mutex protection ([#29247](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29247))
- **Windows platform compatibility** receiving attention with [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust) fixing notty-community ([#29268](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29268)), though challenges remain with miou's poll.h dependency blocking rowex/bancos ([#29251](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29251))
- **CI sandboxing versus real-world testing** tensions emerging with [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube)'s ezcurl tests requiring network access ([#29257](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29257)) and [Matthieu Lemerre](https://github.com/mlemerre)'s codex using curl in builds ([#29259](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29259)), highlighting need for better test isolation strategies
- **Package quality control** becoming more critical with AI-generated submissions like [Jonathan D.A. Jewell](https://github.com/hyperpolymath)'s proven package showing multiple issues from inadequate pre-submission testing ([#29228](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29228))
- **Robur cooperative's Miou ecosystem** rapidly expanding with [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure) releasing flux, carton, bstr, cachet, and proposing rowex/bancos packages focusing on streaming, storage, and concurrency ([#29252](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29252), [#29253](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29253), [#29254](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29254), [#29250](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29250), [#29251](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29251))
- **GCC 15 C23 strictness** causing Docker build failures reported by [Andre](https://github.com/maroneze) with ppx_sexp_conv on OCaml 4.14/Fedora 42, resolved via overlay repository patches ([#29243](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29243))
- **Repository immutability policy** discussion initiated by [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) proposing stricter rules against git URLs in pin-depends to ensure reproducible builds ([#29072](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29072))

repositories.brief_summary: JavaScript parser modernization with ES6+ features including using declarations, import attributes, and defer imports
repositories.new_features_summary: Added support for using/await using declarations, import/export attributes, and import defer syntax
repositories.new_features:
- **JavaScript parser** now supports `using` and `await using` resource management declarations per ECMAScript explicit resource management proposal ([#2143](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2143))
- **Import attributes** added with `import` and `export` statements via `with` clause syntax ([51a6025](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/51a602537d2c212243f46a67f59db16c98e90246), [e2c536c](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/e2c536cd7b0ac5b99c01dc2b24da24696857cefe))
- **Import defer** syntax now supported for deferred namespace imports using `import defer * as x from` ([#2147](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2147))
- **Contextual keywords** `yield` and `await` properly handled in all parsing contexts through virtual token feedback mechanism ([#2148](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2148))
- **Arrow functions in for loops** now parse correctly, fixing issue where arrow functions in variable declarations of for loops were rejected ([#2143](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2143), [#2139](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2139))
- **CLAUDE.md guide** added for AI-assisted development providing project structure, build commands, and architectural overview ([ff087ce](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/ff087cebe0e0127304fa65eaf8bb07e9180fc1e4))
repositories.activity_summary: Parser modernization and testing with two new PRs opened and ongoing WebAssembly optimization work
repositories.activity:
- **Parser improvements** continue with [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) opening new PR for further enhancements ([#2149](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2149)) following up on week's extensive parser refactoring
- **Merged work** includes JavaScript parser modernization aligned with ECMAScript spec ([#2143](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2143)), contextual keyword handling without parser size explosion ([#2148](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2148)), import defer support ([#2147](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2147)), and license file typo fix ([#2145](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2145))
- **WebAssembly optimizations** actively developed by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) with bigstring operations PR showing 2-4x performance improvements in Firefox and Chromium ([#2144](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2144)), string conversion optimizations ([#2124](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2124)), and ongoing WASI runtime implementation ([#1831](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1831))
- **Oxcaml support** continues under development as draft PR with over 11,000 additions affecting 144 files ([#2105](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2105))
repositories.discussions_summary: Performance feedback and tooling discussions for WebAssembly development
repositories.notable_discussions:
- **Bigstring optimization impact** discussed by [@adrien-n](https://github.com/adrien-n) who reported 2-4x speedup on Raspberry Pi 4 with WebAssembly runtime approaching JavaScript baseline performance within 20% compared to previous 400% overhead ([#2144](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2144))
- **WASM tooling challenges** raised by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) regarding difficulties editing WAT files with nested parentheses, poor error reporting, and lack of formatting, with suggestion to use the [wax tool](https://github.com/vouillon/wax) for improved development experience ([#1818](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1818))
- **License typo correction** identified by [Jessica Marz](https://github.com/jkmarz) matching upstream OCaml project's fix, referencing incorrect clause number in LGPL exception text ([#2137](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2137))
repositories.trends_summary: Parser modernization aligned with ECMAScript spec and WebAssembly runtime performance focus
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **ECMAScript spec alignment** accelerating with systematic parser refactoring across 50+ commits restructuring rules to match official spec organization, naming conventions, and precedence handling ([ff087ce](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/ff087cebe0e0127304fa65eaf8bb07e9180fc1e4) through [a2e7172](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/a2e7172dfe27af1dac66e5820dc26ff442466634))
- **ES6+ feature adoption** continuing with modern JavaScript syntax support for resource management, import attributes, and deferred loading patterns addressing real-world library compatibility needs
- **WebAssembly performance optimization** intensifying with focus on bigstring operations, string conversions, and arraybuffer handling showing substantial performance improvements in browser environments ([#2144](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2144), [#2124](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2124))
- **Developer experience improvements** through AI-assisted development documentation and discussion of better tooling for WebAssembly runtime development
repositories.contributors_summary: Primary contributions from hhugo on parser modernization and Jérôme Vouillon on WebAssembly optimizations
repositories.contributors:
- [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) led JavaScript parser modernization with 50+ commits restructuring parser to align with ECMAScript specification, implemented resource management declarations ([#2143](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2143)), contextual keyword handling ([#2148](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2148)), import attributes and defer support ([#2147](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2147)), fixed license typo ([#2145](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2145)), and opened follow-up parser improvements ([#2149](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2149))
- [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) focused on WebAssembly runtime optimization implementing bigstring performance improvements ([#2144](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2144)), string conversion optimizations ([#2124](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2124)), and continuing WASI runtime development ([#1831](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1831)) and Oxcaml support work ([#2105](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2105))
- [@adrien-n](https://github.com/adrien-n) provided performance testing feedback on bigstring optimizations reporting speedup measurements across Firefox and Chromium ([#2144](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2144))
- [Jessica Marz](https://github.com/jkmarz) identified and reported license file typo matching upstream OCaml correction ([#2137](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2137))
- [Spotandjake](https://github.com/spotandjake) reported arrow function parsing bug in for loop contexts while integrating libbinaryen ([#2139](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2139))

repositories.brief_summary: Flambda2 optimization work, ARM64 improvements, modes/modalities for Merlin, and testing infrastructure enhancements
repositories.new_features_summary: Optimized Obj operations, native expect tests, improved mode errors, locality documentation updates, and Flambda2 encyclopedia
repositories.new_features:
- Optimized `Obj.raw_field` and `Obj.set_raw_field` functions for better performance by [James Rayman](https://github.com/jra4), now implemented as OCaml functions rather than external primitives ([#5241](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5241))
- Fixed **symbol projection bugs** affecting unboxed numbers in Flambda2, resolving fatal errors when projecting fields from symbols with unboxed numeric types by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) ([#5299](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5299))
- Fixed **AST iterator** bug that was dropping jkind attributes on type parameters in `-dsource` output ([#5273](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5273), [#5286](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5286))
- Fixed **native toplevel spacing** to match bytecode output, enabling consistent expect test behavior across both modes by [James Rayman](https://github.com/jra4) ([#5243](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5243))
- Added **native expect tests** via new `expect.opt` tool using native toplevel instead of bytecode, with shared functionality in `expectcommon.ml` for running both bytecode and native tests on the same test files by [James Rayman](https://github.com/jra4) ([#5245](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5245))
- Improved **mode error message formatting** using Format's print-box facilities for proper indentation of nested "because" clauses, making error messages more readable by [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) ([#5275](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5275))
- Enhanced **chamelon test minimizer** with new "stub" minimizer that stubs out definitions instead of removing them, plus improved `--test` argument handling and better support for submodules and signatures in `deletelines` by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) ([#5292](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5292), [#5293](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5293), [#5294](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5294), [#5222](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5222))
- Made **ocaml-jit platform independent** by refactoring to not be tied to x86 binary emitter, enabling operation with ARM64 by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) ([#5186](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5186))
- Updated **locality documentation** to be less coupled to stack allocation, clarifying that local values may be stack allocated but this is not guaranteed, and explaining mode crossing behavior for types like `int` ([#5274](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5274))
- Added comprehensive **Flambda2 optimizations encyclopedia** documenting CPS conversion, canonicalization, unboxing, variant unboxing, and match optimizations with examples showing before/after transformations ([#5082](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5082))
repositories.activity_summary: Type system work on modes/modalities/borrowing, Flambda2 improvements, ARM64 SIMD vectors, and backend refactoring
repositories.activity:
- Nine **new PRs opened** including improved Merlin jkind printing by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) ([#5304](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5304)), 64-bit `.eh_frame` support for dissector by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) ([#5303](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5303)), refactored uniqueness analysis errors by [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) ([#5302](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5302)), small int conversions by [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) ([#5305](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5305)), ARM wide vector translation by [Max Slater](https://github.com/TheNumbat) ([#5291](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5291)), signature default modalities per axis by [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) ([#5290](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5290)), and DragonFly BSD build fixes (WIP, breaks Linux) by [Michael Neumann](https://github.com/mneumann) ([#5282](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5282))
- **Modes and modalities** work advanced through multiple merged PRs by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) adding mode/modality locations to typedtree for Merlin syntax hovers ([#5218](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5218)), testing mode annotations make it through to typedtree ([#5297](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5297)), and exposing actual mode in `Texp_ident` for variable reference tooltips ([#5300](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5300))
- **Borrowing support** PR from [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) remains active with ongoing discussion about error messages for out-of-context borrowing and implementation scope, representing simplified uniqueness via `borrow_` operator for temporary aliased access ([#5215](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5215))
- **Flambda2 backend refactoring** moved `alloc_mode` out of `call_kind` by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs) in preparation for zero-alloc changes ([#5287](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5287)), merged `Pmakemixedblock` into `Pmakeblock` as minimum required for layout polymorphism by [Jack Rickard](https://github.com/Skepfyr) ([#5238](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5238)), and introduced `Compilation_unit.full_path_with_arguments_as_string` refactoring by [@shym](https://github.com/shym) ([#5289](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5289))
- **ARM64 and SIMD** development including wide vector translation to tuples on ARM (vec256 → vec128 & vec128) enabling 256-bit SIMD tests by [Max Slater](https://github.com/TheNumbat) ([#5291](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5291)), arrays of unboxed pairs of vec128s as prerequisite ([#5239](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5239)), binary emitter verification with new `-verify-binary-emitter` option ([#5198](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5198)), and extra owee functionality with extensive review feedback from ChatGPT 5.1 Codex identifying relocation issues ([#5188](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5188))
- **Type system infrastructure** advanced with shape constructors for unknown types in preparation for improved layouts in debug information by [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon) ([#4928](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4928)), instantiated type declaration parameters in `Typeopt` avoiding `caml_modify` by [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) ([#5108](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5108)), and ongoing work on scannable axes for nullability/separability ([#5169](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5169))
- **Backend and register allocation** improvements with IRC interference graph extraction to dedicated module ([#5237](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5237)) and bit matrix representation exploration for performance benchmarking ([#5296](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5296)) by [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc)
- **Developer tooling** updates including Claude AI agent project settings ([#5281](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5281)), sandboxing experimentation (disabled by default due to git worktree issues) ([#5284](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5284)), and build output cleanup that was reverted due to macOS compatibility with `cp --remove-destination` ([#5285](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5285), [#5301](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5301))
- **Ongoing feature development** includes new reversible name mangling scheme ([#5099](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5099)), unsigned division primitives ([#4936](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4936)), fexpr primitive representation rework ([#5221](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5221)), type-level eval experiments ([#5288](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5288)), annotating polymorphic applications under quotes ([#5154](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5154)), phantom variable DWARF support ([#4595](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4595)), improved layouts for debug information ([#4916](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4916)), and faster oldify for minor collector performance ([#5163](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5163))
repositories.trends_summary: Focus on Flambda2 upstreaming preparation, Seamm backend planning, ARM64 platform maturity, and developer tooling
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Flambda2 upstreaming preparation** intensified with [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) creating 32 organizational issues covering match-in-match optimization ([#5269](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5269), [#4696](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4696)), lifted constant handling improvements ([#5295](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5295), [#5254](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5254)), zero-alloc analysis enhancements ([#5278](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5278), [#5276](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5276), [#5277](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5277)), the reaper local value slot analysis ([#5267](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5267), [#4694](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4694)), function specialization ([#5266](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5266), [#1525](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/1525)), and general upstreaming preparation ([#5272](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5272))
- **Seamm backend initiative** launched with 11 new tracking issues covering control flow language definition ([#5257](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5257)), Flambda 2 to Seamm translation ([#5258](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5258)), Seamm to Cfg translation ([#5259](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5259)), (A)Egraph core library and extraction ([#5260](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5260), [#5261](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5261)), rewrite engine ([#5262](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5262)), Cmm to Seamm translation as transition measure ([#5263](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5263)), thinking about AEgraphs vs Egraphs ([#5264](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5264)), sprint preparations ([#5265](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5265)), and rewrite rule structure considerations ([#5256](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5256)), with prototype work tracked in ([#5251](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5251))
- **Testing infrastructure expansion** with native expect tests enabling consistent testing across bytecode and native modes ([#5245](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5245)), design docs for improved Flambda2 testing ([#5271](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5271), [#4699](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4699)), and backend testing improvements ([#5268](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5268), [#5253](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5253))
- **ARM64 platform maturity** advancing through platform-independent JIT refactoring ([#5186](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5186)), wide vector support via tuple translation ([#5291](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5291), [#5239](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5239)), binary emitter verification infrastructure ([#5198](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5198)), and enhanced owee library functionality ([#5188](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5188))
- **Developer experience and AI tooling** seeing experimentation with Claude AI agent integration through project settings ([#5281](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5281)), sandboxing attempts ([#5284](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5284)), and build output cleanup efforts ([#5285](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5285)), alongside chamelon minimizer enhancements ([#5292](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5292), [#5293](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5293), [#5294](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5294))
- **Modes and modalities tooling** enhanced for better Merlin integration with typedtree locations ([#5218](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5218)), jkind printing improvements ([#5304](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5304)), and actual mode exposure ([#5300](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5300)), supporting interactive development with modes
- **DWARF and debugging** infrastructure building toward production-ready debugging with phantom variable support ([#4595](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4595)), improved layout handling ([#4916](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4916)), shape constructors for unknown types ([#4928](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4928)), debug location tracking for partial applications ([#5225](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5225)), and general debugging infrastructure tracking ([#5270](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5270), [#5280](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5280))

repositories.brief_summary: New `locate-type-multi` command merged plus two PRs opened for improved type navigation
repositories.new_features_summary: New `locate-type-multi` command for navigating complex type definitions
repositories.new_features:
- New `locate-type-multi` command enables tree-based navigation of complex type definitions, allowing users to jump to definitions of all type components in expressions like `int list` or `(a * b) -> c` ([#148](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/148))
- Protocol extension documented in PROTOCOL.md describing the new command's tree-based response structure for representing nested type references ([ea4459144aaf6603de42b15a1461d1dbeb538237](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/commit/ea4459144aaf6603de42b15a1461d1dbeb538237))
- New API in `src/analysis/locate_types.mli` exposing `create_type_tree` function that converts OCaml type expressions into simplified tree representations ([ea4459144aaf6603de42b15a1461d1dbeb538237](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/commit/ea4459144aaf6603de42b15a1461d1dbeb538237))

group_summaries.brief_summary: Active package releases with 29 merges including new tools, Dune 3.21 announcement published, and three OCaml tooling tutorials opened
group_summaries.new_features_summary: New packages: opam-repomin, csexp-query, ortools bindings, domainpc multicore utilities; Dune 3.21 with OxCaml library parameters
group_summaries.new_features:
- **opam-repomin 1.0.0** by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm): Minimizes opam repository overlays into unified repos using opam-0install solver to determine minimum package sets ([ocaml/opam-repository#29274](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29274))
- **csexp-query 0.1.0** by [Josh Berdine](https://github.com/jberdine): Query tool for canonical s-expressions with field, index, each, pipe, cat, and this operations ([ocaml/opam-repository#29273](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29273))
- **ortools 9.14.0** by [Timothy Bourke](https://github.com/tbrk): OCaml bindings for Google OR-Tools models and CP-SAT solver, initial release with incomplete interface ([ocaml/opam-repository#29159](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29159))
- **domainpc 0.1** by [Hichem R. A.](https://github.com/hra687261): Spawns OCaml domains ensuring they run on separate cores for better multicore performance ([ocaml/opam-repository#29223](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29223))
- **dead_code_analyzer 1.1.0** by [Corentin De Souza](https://github.com/fantazio): LexiFi's dead code analyzer for OCaml projects ([ocaml/opam-repository#29262](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29262))
- **Dune 3.21.0 release announcement** published documenting experimental OxCaml library parameters, horizontal scrolling in TUI, improved cram test error messages with shell commands, copy-on-write file copying on Linux, and support for Tangled ATproto repositories ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3453](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3453))
- **qdrant 0.2.0** by [Jeong-Sik Yun](https://github.com/jeong-sik): OCaml client library for Qdrant vector database ([ocaml/opam-repository#29267](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29267))
- **restricted 1.2.0** by [@stopusingghpls](https://github.com/stopusingghpls): Linux filesystem sandboxing via landlock(7), added semantic versioning, tested on x86_64-openbsd and Linux with Landlock ABI v7 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29233](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29233))
- **slipshow 0.8.0** by [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd): Presentation tool with mirror mode for dual displays, external file support, and documentation rework ([ocaml/opam-repository#29249](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29249))
- **lmdb 1.1** by [@madroach](https://github.com/madroach): Fixed bad allocation pattern causing segfaults, deprecated old iterators, added Map.close and Map.to_dispenser iterator implementation ([ocaml/opam-repository#29212](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29212))
- **liquidsoap 2.4.2** by [Romain Beauxis](https://github.com/toots): Savonet audio streaming tool update covering liquidsoap, liquidsoap-js, liquidsoap-lang, and liquidsoap-mode packages ([ocaml/opam-repository#29235](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29235))
- **multicore-magic 2.3.2** by [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic): Low-level multicore utilities with unboxed Atomic_array support on OCaml 5.4 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29241](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29241))
