group_summaries.brief_summary: Extensive code modernization across Dune and js_of_ocaml, critical MirageOS bug fix, and enhanced editor integration capabilities
group_summaries.new_features_summary: Git commit hash locking, MirageOS select field fix, FD leak fix, JS ES6+ support, editor type navigation enhancements
group_summaries.new_features:
- [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune): **Git repository locking** now records commit hashes when locking git repositories, improving build reproducibility ([#13297](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13297)) by [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV)
- [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune): Fixed **select field evaluation** bug that broke all MirageOS unikernels after lwt 6.0.0, properly evaluating closure when resolving select fields with unavailable optional dependencies ([#13389](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13389), [#13299](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13299)) by [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro)
- [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune): Fixed **file descriptor leak** causing "too many open files" errors by adding missing `O_CLOEXEC` to redirect files ([#13385](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13385), [#13379](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13379)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune): **Option to skip test dependencies** when building packages, avoiding unnecessary test dependency compilation ([#13298](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13298), [#12658](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/12658)) by [Sudha Parimala](https://github.com/Sudha247)
- [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune): **Detection of invalid git repositories** to prevent permanent bricking when git-repo folder is corrupt ([#13398](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13398)) by [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV)
- [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune): **Windows PATH handling improvements** using Env_path helper for correct PATH separator (: vs ;) ([#13405](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13405), [#12433](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/12433)) by [Puneeth Chaganti](https://github.com/punchagan)
- [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune): **Relocatable compiler support** for OCaml 5.5 by resolving symlinks from install actions, enabling better caching ([#13239](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13239), [#13229](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13229)) by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter)
- [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune): **Enhanced tracing capabilities** including diff category tags, thread spawning events, promotion registration events, process queue information, and metrics for digesting/reading/directories ([#13451](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13451), [#13445](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13445), [#13444](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13444), [#13446](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13446), [#13382](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13382), [#13387](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13387)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune): **Emacs integration improvement** with new `dune-find-dominating` command to find dominating dune file ([#12696](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/12696)) by [@arvidj](https://github.com/arvidj)
- [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin): **locate-types command** now includes an `Other` variant in results, enabling editors to display type variables and other non-navigable types more accurately ([#2025](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2025)) by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923)
- [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin): **Optional parameter handling** improved in locate-types by stripping the `option` wrapper from optional parameters, so `?foo:int` now shows `int` instead of `int option` ([#2027](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2027)) by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923)
- [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin): **Record field autocompletion** fixed by adding `for_completion` field to reader cache fingerprint, resolving issue where completion after a dot produced no field candidates ([#2028](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2028)) by [Kostia Balytskyi](https://github.com/ikostia)
- [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml): **JavaScript parser** now supports `using` and `await using` resource management declarations per ECMAScript explicit resource management proposal ([#2143](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2143)) by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo)
- [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml): **Import attributes** added with `import` and `export` statements via `with` clause syntax ([51a6025](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/51a602537d2c212243f46a67f59db16c98e90246), [e2c536c](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/e2c536cd7b0ac5b99c01dc2b24da24696857cefe)) by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo)
- [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml): **Import defer** syntax now supported for deferred namespace imports using `import defer * as x from` ([#2147](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2147)) by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo)
- [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml): **Contextual keywords** `yield` and `await` properly handled in all parsing contexts through virtual token feedback mechanism ([#2148](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2148)) by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo)
- [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml): **Arrow functions in for loops** now parse correctly, fixing issue where arrow functions in variable declarations of for loops were rejected ([#2143](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2143), [#2139](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2139)) by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo)
group_summaries.group_overview:
- **High-volume week** with 50+ merged PRs in [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) and 50+ commits in [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml), focused on code modernization and specification alignment
- **Critical bug fixes** including MirageOS-breaking select field issue in [ocaml/dune#13389](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13389) and file descriptor leak in [ocaml/dune#13385](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13385)
- **Editor integration improvements** spanning [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin) type navigation enhancements and [ocaml/ocaml-lsp](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp) custom request expansion
- **Platform support expansion** with Windows compatibility fixes in [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) ([#13405](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13405), [#13293](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13293)) and [ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam) ([#6847](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6847))
- **Minimal activity** in [ocaml/ocamlfind](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind), [ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc), [ocaml/opam-file-format](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-file-format), and [ocaml-dune/spawn](https://github.com/ocaml-dune/spawn) repositories with no commits this week
group_summaries.activity_summary: Code consolidation in Dune, parser specification alignment in js_of_ocaml, OxCaml integration progress, Windows support improvements
group_summaries.activity:
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) refactoring effort** led by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) with 35+ merged PRs consolidating scattered modules including moving promotion back to engine ([#13440](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13440)), splitting Path.Local into own module ([#13436](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13436)), moving all diff programs to one module ([#13452](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13452)), extracting directory traversals ([#13448](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13448)), and merging file_watcher into scheduler ([#13416](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13416))
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) OxCaml integration** progressing with parameterised library split for JSOO ([#13413](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13413)) merged by [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w), JSOO support PR opened ([#13414](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13414)), workspace file support ([#13395](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13395)) and .cms shape files ([#13397](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13397)) by [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon), and compilation context improvements ([#13346](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13346)) by [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro)
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) Windows support** advancing with shebang parsing for shell script execution ([#13293](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13293)) and PATH separator fixes ([#13405](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13405)) by [Puneeth Chaganti](https://github.com/punchagan), plus ocamlfind .exe extensions work ([#12430](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/12430)) tracked under Windows umbrella issue ([#11161](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/11161))
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) parser modernization** with 50+ commits by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) restructuring parser to align with ECMAScript specification, improving maintainability and adding ES6+ features ([#2143](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2143), [#2148](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2148), [#2147](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2147))
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) WebAssembly optimization** by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) implementing bigstring performance improvements showing 2-4x speedup ([#2144](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2144)), string conversion optimizations ([#2124](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2124)), and continuing WASI runtime work ([#1831](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1831))
- **[ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin) region-based queries** proposed by [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) with new `-stop` flag for `enclosing` command to enable region-based queries instead of point-based ([#2029](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2029)), addressing LSP protocol limitations with OCaml's expression-heavy structure
- **[ocaml/ocaml-lsp](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp) custom request expansion** with [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) opening two PRs for merlin-locate-types functionality ([#1584](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1584)) and destruct custom request for ocaml-eglot integration ([#1583](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1583))
- **[ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam) documentation improvements** with [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust) documenting raw-field syntax more prominently ([#6846](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6846)) and [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb) adding formal MAINTAINERS file ([#6845](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6845), [#6844](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6844))
- **[ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin) OCaml 5.5 support** tracked by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) to monitor progress as OCaml 5.5 approaches its freeze ([#2024](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2024))
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- **Editor integration stack** improvements coordinated across [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin) and [ocaml/ocaml-lsp](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp), with [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) enhancing locate-types in Merlin ([#2025](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2025), [#2027](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2027)) and [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) exposing it via custom requests in ocaml-lsp ([#1584](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1584))
- **OxCaml ecosystem integration** progressing across [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) and [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml), with [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) implementing parameterised library support in Dune ([#13413](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13413), [#13414](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13414)) and [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) continuing Oxcaml support in js_of_ocaml ([#2105](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2105))
- **Windows platform support** addressed systematically in [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) ([#13405](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13405), [#13293](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13293), [#11161](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/11161)) and [ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam) ([#6847](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6847)), with [Puneeth Chaganti](https://github.com/punchagan) working on Dune-side issues
- **OCaml 5.5 preparation** tracked in [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin) ([#2024](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2024)) and [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) with relocatable compiler support ([#13239](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13239)) by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter)
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- **MirageOS critical bug** reported by [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) in [ocaml/dune#13299](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13299) where all MirageOS unikernels stopped building after lwt 6.0.0, resolved by [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro) fixing select field evaluation ([#13389](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13389))
- **Region-based query support** discussed in [ocaml/merlin#2029](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2029) where [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) and [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) explored expression selection handling in LSP, with [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) suggesting alternative naming and noting similar functionality in `type-enclosing`
- **Bigstring optimization impact** discussed in [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2144](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2144) with [@adrien-n](https://github.com/adrien-n) reporting 2-4x speedup on Raspberry Pi 4, bringing WebAssembly runtime within 20% of JavaScript baseline performance compared to previous 400% overhead
- **Windows installation failure** in [ocaml/opam#6847](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6847) with [@WellDoneYouWin](https://github.com/WellDoneYouWin) reporting curl SSL handshake error on Windows/Cygwin, diagnosed by [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli) as network connectivity or certificate issue
- **0install solver behavior** discussed in [ocaml/opam#6843](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6843) by [@tobil4sk](https://github.com/tobil4sk) where builtin-0install installs both i686 and x86_64 mingw variants simultaneously for conf-zlib and conf-libpcre2-8, unlike builtin-mccs+glpk which installs only x86_64
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Code consolidation and architecture cleanup** with [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) leading extensive refactoring in [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) to merge scattered modules, standardize error messages, and simplify internal architecture across 35+ PRs ([#13440](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13440), [#13436](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13436), [#13452](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13452))
- **Specification alignment efforts** with [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) restructuring [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) parser to match ECMAScript specification organization across 50+ commits, improving long-term maintainability and enabling systematic ES6+ feature additions
- **Enhanced editor integration** across the toolchain with locate-types improvements in [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin) ([#2025](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2025), [#2027](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2027)), custom request expansion in [ocaml/ocaml-lsp](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp) ([#1584](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1584), [#1583](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1583)), and Emacs integration in [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) ([#12696](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/12696))
- **Windows platform support expansion** gaining traction with systematic addressing of platform-specific issues in [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) including PATH separators ([#13405](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13405)), shell script execution ([#13293](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13293)), and .exe handling ([#12430](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/12430)) under umbrella issue ([#11161](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/11161))
- **Build reproducibility focus** in [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) with git commit hash locking ([#13297](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13297)), relocatable compiler support for OCaml 5.5 ([#13239](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13239)), and rev-store validation ([#13398](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13398))
- **WebAssembly performance optimization** intensifying in [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) with bigstring operations showing 2-4x improvements ([#2144](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2144)) and ongoing WASI runtime implementation ([#1831](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1831))

group_summaries.brief_summary: Flambda2 upstreaming organization, Merlin IDE enhancements, ARM64 maturity, and package ecosystem integration
group_summaries.new_features_summary: Native expect tests, type navigation, optimized primitives, mode error formatting, and Flambda2 documentation
group_summaries.new_features:
- **oxcaml/oxcaml**: Optimized `Obj.raw_field` and `Obj.set_raw_field` functions for better performance by [James Rayman](https://github.com/jra4), now implemented as OCaml functions rather than external primitives ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5241](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5241))
- **oxcaml/oxcaml**: Fixed symbol projection bugs affecting unboxed numbers in Flambda2, resolving fatal errors when projecting fields from symbols with unboxed numeric types by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5299](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5299))
- **oxcaml/oxcaml**: Added native expect tests via new `expect.opt` tool using native toplevel instead of bytecode, with shared functionality in `expectcommon.ml` for running both bytecode and native tests on the same test files by [James Rayman](https://github.com/jra4) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5245](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5245))
- **oxcaml/oxcaml**: Improved mode error message formatting using Format's print-box facilities for proper indentation of nested "because" clauses, making error messages more readable by [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5275](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5275))
- **oxcaml/oxcaml**: Enhanced chamelon test minimizer with new "stub" minimizer that stubs out definitions instead of removing them, plus improved `--test` argument handling and better support for submodules and signatures in `deletelines` by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5292](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5292), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5293](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5293), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5294](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5294))
- **oxcaml/oxcaml**: Made ocaml-jit platform independent by refactoring to not be tied to x86 binary emitter, enabling operation with ARM64 by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5186](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5186))
- **oxcaml/oxcaml**: Updated locality documentation to be less coupled to stack allocation, clarifying that local values may be stack allocated but this is not guaranteed, and explaining mode crossing behavior for types like `int` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5274](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5274))
- **oxcaml/oxcaml**: Added comprehensive Flambda2 optimizations encyclopedia documenting CPS conversion, canonicalization, unboxing, variant unboxing, and match optimizations with examples showing before/after transformations ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5082](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5082))
- **oxcaml/merlin**: New `locate-type-multi` command enables tree-based navigation of complex type definitions, allowing users to jump to definitions of all type components in expressions like `int list` or `(a * b) -> c` by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) ([oxcaml/merlin#148](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/148))
- **oxcaml/merlin**: Protocol extension documented in PROTOCOL.md describing the new command's tree-based response structure for representing nested type references ([oxcaml/merlin commit ea4459144aaf6603de42b15a1461d1dbeb538237](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/commit/ea4459144aaf6603de42b15a1461d1dbeb538237))
group_summaries.activity_summary: Major organizational work on Flambda2 upstreaming, Seamm backend planning, modes/modalities integration, and package ecosystem improvements
group_summaries.activity:
- **oxcaml/oxcaml**: [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) created 32 organizational issues for Flambda2 upstreaming preparation, covering match-in-match optimization ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5269](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5269)), lifted constant handling ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5295](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5295)), zero-alloc analysis enhancements ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5278](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5278)), the reaper local value slot analysis ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5267](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5267)), and function specialization ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5266](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5266))
- **oxcaml/oxcaml**: Seamm backend initiative launched with 11 new tracking issues covering control flow language definition ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5257](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5257)), Flambda 2 to Seamm translation ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5258](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5258)), (A)Egraph core library and extraction ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5260](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5260), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5261](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5261)), rewrite engine ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5262](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5262)), and Cmm to Seamm translation ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5263](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5263))
- **oxcaml/oxcaml**: Modes and modalities work advanced with [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) adding mode/modality locations to typedtree for Merlin syntax hovers ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5218](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5218)), testing mode annotations in typedtree ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5297](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5297)), and exposing actual mode in `Texp_ident` for variable reference tooltips ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5300](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5300))
- **oxcaml/oxcaml**: ARM64 and SIMD development with [Max Slater](https://github.com/TheNumbat) advancing wide vector translation to tuples enabling 256-bit SIMD tests ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5291](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5291)), arrays of unboxed vec128 pairs ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5239](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5239)), and binary emitter verification infrastructure ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5198](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5198))
- **oxcaml/oxcaml**: Flambda2 backend refactoring moved `alloc_mode` out of `call_kind` by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5287](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5287)) and merged `Pmakemixedblock` into `Pmakeblock` by [Jack Rickard](https://github.com/Skepfyr) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5238](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5238))
- **oxcaml/oxcaml**: [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc) worked on IRC register allocator improvements with interference graph extraction ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5237](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5237)) and bit matrix representation ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5296](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5296))
- **oxcaml/opam-repository**: [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) opened PR to enable all oxcaml packages to be coinstalled using the new opam-repomin plugin, fixing missing zarith.1.14+ox dependencies and allowing lwt.6 alongside the oxcaml compiler ([oxcaml/opam-repository#32](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/pull/32))
- **oxcaml/opam-repository**: [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) proposed js_of_ocaml integration using the latest oxcaml branch to fix issues with parameterised libs required for incremental compilation in [ocaml/dune#13414](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13414) ([oxcaml/opam-repository#31](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/pull/31))
- **oxcaml/merlin**: [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) opened two PRs for kind-enclosing query addition ([oxcaml/merlin#208](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/208)) and cherry-picking upstream locate-types improvements ([oxcaml/merlin#207](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/207)) with oxcaml-specific adaptations
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- **Modes/modalities IDE integration**: Coordinated work between [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) and [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin) to expose type system information for IDE tooling, with [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) adding typedtree locations in the compiler ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5218](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5218), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5300](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5300)) and implementing corresponding Merlin queries ([oxcaml/merlin#208](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/208))
- **js_of_ocaml ecosystem integration**: [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w)'s PR in [oxcaml/opam-repository#31](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/pull/31) proposes using the oxcaml branch of js_of_ocaml to address parameterised libs issues affecting [ocaml/dune#13414](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13414), improving compatibility between oxcaml and dune's incremental compilation
- **Package ecosystem coinstallability**: [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm)'s work in [oxcaml/opam-repository#32](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/pull/32) enables oxcaml packages to coexist with standard OCaml packages like lwt.6, facilitated by the opam-repomin plugin for managing the oxcaml ecosystem as a unified collection
- **Upstream Merlin integration**: [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin) continues cherry-picking features from [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin), including `locate-type-multi` from [ocaml/merlin#1951](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1951) and locate-types improvements from [ocaml/merlin#2025](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2025) and [ocaml/merlin#2027](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2027), with adaptations for oxcaml's type system differences ([oxcaml/merlin#207](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/207))
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) provided review feedback on [oxcaml/opam-repository#23](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/pull/23) noting that uutf 1.0.3 conflicts with cmdliner >= 2.0, requiring resolution before the package updates can proceed
- [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn)'s borrowing support PR ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5215](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5215)) sparked ongoing discussion about error messages for out-of-context borrowing and implementation scope for simplified uniqueness via the `borrow_` operator
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Flambda2 upstreaming preparation** intensified with [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) creating 32 organizational issues to structure work on match-in-match optimization ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5269](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5269)), lifted constant handling ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5295](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5295)), zero-alloc analysis ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5278](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5278)), the reaper local value slot analysis ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5267](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5267)), and function specialization ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5266](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5266))
- **Seamm backend initiative** launched with 11 tracking issues planning a new compiler backend based on egraphs, including control flow language definition ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5257](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5257)), translation layers ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5258](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5258), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5259](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5259)), (A)Egraph core library ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5260](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5260), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5261](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5261)), and rewrite engine ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5262](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5262))
- **ARM64 platform maturity** advancing through platform-independent JIT refactoring ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5186](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5186)), wide vector support via tuple translation ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5291](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5291)), binary emitter verification infrastructure ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5198](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5198)), and enhanced owee library functionality ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5188](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5188))
- **IDE and developer experience improvements** with coordinated work across [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) and [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin) for modes/modalities tooling ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5218](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5218), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5300](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5300), [oxcaml/merlin#208](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/208)), type navigation enhancements ([oxcaml/merlin#148](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/148)), and test minimizer improvements ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5292](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5292))
- **Package ecosystem integration** focusing on making oxcaml packages coinstallable with standard OCaml packages ([oxcaml/opam-repository#32](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/pull/32)) and adopting public branches of tools like js_of_ocaml rather than maintaining separate patches ([oxcaml/opam-repository#31](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/pull/31))
- **Testing infrastructure expansion** with native expect tests enabling consistent testing across bytecode and native modes ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5245](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5245)), design docs for improved Flambda2 testing ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5271](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5271)), and backend testing improvements ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5268](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5268))

group_summaries.brief_summary: String module enhancements merged, persistent compiler RFC proposed, type system soundness fixes, and FreeBSD profiling support added
group_summaries.new_features_summary: String split/replace functions, Gc.Tweak runtime tuning, FreeBSD profiling support, deterministic compilation, and AI contribution guidelines
group_summaries.new_features:
- **String module enhancements** with `split_first`, `split_last`, `split_all`, and `rsplit_all` functions for splitting strings by separator ([ocaml/ocaml#14437](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14437)) plus `replace_first`, `replace_last`, and `replace_all` for substring replacement ([ocaml/ocaml#14436](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14436)) by [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli)
- **Gc.Tweak module** providing named GC parameters accessible via `Gc.Tweak.set "foo" 42` or `OCAMLRUNPARAM='Xfoo=42'` for GC experimentation and tuning ([ocaml/ocaml#14367](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14367)) by [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan) and Nick Barnes
- **FreeBSD frame pointer support** for AMD64 and ARM64 platforms enabling profiling with `pmcstat` and `dtrace`, with documentation in the manual ([ocaml/ocaml#14486](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14486)) by [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist)
- **Deterministic compilation** restored by resetting type ID and level counters between compilations, making `ocamlc -c foo.mli foo.ml` produce identical output to separate invocations ([ocaml/ocaml#14483](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14483)) by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27)
- **Windows minimum version** now explicitly set to Windows 8/Server 2012 via `_WIN32_WINNT` macro defined consistently across codebase ([ocaml/ocaml#14484](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14484)) by [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA), fixing [ocaml/ocaml#7418](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/7418)
- **AI contribution guidelines** added in AI.md and CONTRIBUTING.md requiring disclosure of substantial AI authorship ([ocaml/ocaml#14052](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14052), [ocaml/ocaml#14383](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14383)) by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) and [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell)
group_summaries.group_overview:
- The core group saw significant progress on stdlib enhancements with multiple string manipulation functions merged to [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) ([ocaml/ocaml#14437](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14437), [ocaml/ocaml#14436](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14436)), new GC tuning capabilities via `Gc.Tweak` ([ocaml/ocaml#14367](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14367)), and expanded platform support for FreeBSD profiling ([ocaml/ocaml#14486](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14486))
- Type system reliability received attention with soundness fixes for polymorphic patterns ([ocaml/ocaml#14480](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14480), [ocaml/ocaml#14479](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14479)) and shape occurrence handling ([ocaml/ocaml#14476](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14476)), while deterministic compilation was restored ([ocaml/ocaml#14483](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14483))
- In [ocaml/RFCs](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs), [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) proposed a persistent compiler processes RFC ([ocaml/RFCs#60](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/60)) aimed at reducing build times by keeping compiler instances running across invocations, generating discussion about protocol design and performance expectations
- Six new PRs opened in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) including Power backend CFI support ([ocaml/ocaml#14482](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14482)), additional string manipulation functions ([ocaml/ocaml#14487](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14487)), and internal refactoring for C++ compatibility ([ocaml/ocaml#14490](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14490))
- Several proposals were closed without merge, including the unstable attribute mechanism ([ocaml/ocaml#14430](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14430)) after developer meeting showed no consensus on ecosystem implications
group_summaries.activity_summary: Six new PRs opened for type system fixes and platform support, with ongoing reviews of lazy thunks and continuation optimizations
group_summaries.activity:
- **Six new PRs opened** in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) including soundness fix for polymorphic parameters by [Alistair](https://github.com/johnyob) ([ocaml/ocaml#14480](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14480)) addressing [ocaml/ocaml#14434](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14434), alternative fix by [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan) ([ocaml/ocaml#14479](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14479)), Power backend CFI support by [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist) ([ocaml/ocaml#14482](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14482)), ocp-indent formatting configuration by [Alistair](https://github.com/johnyob) ([ocaml/ocaml#14485](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14485)), and proposed `String.drop_prefix` additions by Michael Neumann ([ocaml/ocaml#14487](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14487))
- **Runtime and compiler improvements** in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) delivered through opcode synchronization simplification eliminating `jumptbl.h` and `opnames.h` using X-Macro pattern ([ocaml/ocaml#14488](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14488)), runtime header include fixes allowing independent inclusion ([ocaml/ocaml#14489](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14489)), and C++ compatibility improvements suppressing flexible array member warnings ([ocaml/ocaml#14490](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14490)) by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27)
- **Shape occurrences bug fix** resolving assertion failure in `env.ml` by extending `Env.find_uid` to handle extended paths for constructors and labels ([ocaml/ocaml#14476](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14476)) by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron), fixing [ocaml/ocaml#14474](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14474)
- **Internal refactoring** in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) using C2y `_Countof` operator for array size calculations with type safety ([ocaml/ocaml#14413](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14413)) and labeling boolean argument in `typing/env.ml` for readability ([ocaml/ocaml#14477](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14477)) by [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) and [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche)
- **Opam package management** updated with missing conflict declaration for compiler packages in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) ([ocaml/ocaml#14475](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14475)) by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate)
- **PRs closed without merge** in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) including unstable attribute proposal ([ocaml/ocaml#14430](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14430)) after developer meeting showed no consensus on ecosystem implications, automated stdlib labeling workflow ([ocaml/ocaml#14467](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14467)) replaced by giving stdlib team triaging rights, and constructor redefinition warning ([ocaml/ocaml#12312](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/12312)) closed per consensus in [ocaml/ocaml#10655](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/10655)
- **Ongoing review activity** in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) on mutex-protected lazy thunks proposal ([ocaml/ocaml#14393](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14393)) by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche), `Fun.todo` addition approved by stdlib team ([ocaml/ocaml#13101](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13101)) by Pavlo Khrystenko, continuation representation optimization ([ocaml/ocaml#14469](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14469)) by [KC Sivaramakrishnan](https://github.com/kayceesrk), labeled tuple projections ([ocaml/ocaml#14257](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14257)) by [Alistair](https://github.com/johnyob), and arm64 miscompilation fix ([ocaml/ocaml#14010](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14010)) by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell)
- **Persistent compiler RFC discussion** in [ocaml/RFCs](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs) generated feedback about implementation details including protocol design suggestions from [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) for potential Merlin integration, performance expectations from [Alain Frisch](https://github.com/alainfrisch) noting 10-20% gains justify the proposal, and I/O handling approaches recommended by [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli) ([ocaml/RFCs#60](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/60))
- **Labeled tuple projections RFC** in [ocaml/RFCs](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs) continued with ongoing debate about tuple polymorphism, layout normalization, and the scope of the proposal ([ocaml/RFCs#57](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/57)) by [Alistair](https://github.com/johnyob)
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli) contributed both String module enhancements to [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) ([ocaml/ocaml#14437](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14437), [ocaml/ocaml#14436](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14436)) and provided extensive review feedback on the persistent compiler RFC in [ocaml/RFCs](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs) ([ocaml/RFCs#60](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/60)) regarding protocol design and I/O handling
- [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) authored the persistent compiler processes RFC in [ocaml/RFCs](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs) ([ocaml/RFCs#60](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/60)) while actively reviewing multiple PRs in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) including String module additions, stdlib proposals, and runtime changes
- [Alistair](https://github.com/johnyob) is driving work on both labeled tuple projections RFC in [ocaml/RFCs](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs) ([ocaml/RFCs#57](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/57)) and the corresponding implementation in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) ([ocaml/ocaml#14257](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14257)), while also opening soundness fixes for polymorphic patterns ([ocaml/ocaml#14480](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14480))
- Build performance and reproducibility themes connected across both repositories, with deterministic compilation improvements in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) ([ocaml/ocaml#14483](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14483)) complementing the persistent compiler RFC in [ocaml/RFCs](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs) ([ocaml/RFCs#60](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/60)) aimed at reducing compilation times
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- **String module enhancement proposal** by [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli) presenting comprehensive API additions including index search with predicates, substring searching/replacing, and string breaking operations using consistent `first/last/all` naming convention in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) ([ocaml/ocaml#14137](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14137))
- **Non-empty list type** proposal by Mathieu Barbin suggesting adding type definition without API to stdlib for third-party library compatibility: `type 'a t = ( :: ) of 'a * 'a list` in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) ([ocaml/ocaml#14261](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14261))
- **Signal handler SA_RESTART flag** discussion initiated by Christophe Raffalli proposing default use of SA_RESTART to reduce EINTR handling complexity in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) ([ocaml/ocaml#14481](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14481))
- **Unstable features mechanism** debated in developer meeting regarding [ocaml/ocaml#14430](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14430) with concerns about ecosystem fragmentation and maintenance burden leading to no consensus
- **Domain.DLS documentation** improvements requested across multiple issues in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) ([ocaml/ocaml#11905](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/11905), [ocaml/ocaml#11920](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/11920), [ocaml/ocaml#11921](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/11921)) highlighting need for better examples and performance guidance
- **Persistent compiler protocol design** debated in [ocaml/RFCs](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs) between captured output approach (used by Bazel) versus file-based stdio redirection (used by Buck2), with concerns about temporary file overhead on Windows raised by [Alain Frisch](https://github.com/alainfrisch) ([ocaml/RFCs#60](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/60))
- **Performance expectations** for persistent mode discussed in [ocaml/RFCs](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs) with [Alain Frisch](https://github.com/alainfrisch) arguing 10-20% compilation gains justify the relatively lightweight proposal, while [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli) noted gains primarily affect cold builds rather than cached incremental builds ([ocaml/RFCs#60](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/60))
- **Developer meeting consensus** on persistent compiler RFC in [ocaml/RFCs](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs) indicates interest but requires caching `.cmi` loading experiments to demonstrate sufficient performance gains beyond the 10-20% baseline, with careful attention to reproducibility concerns around internal object IDs ([ocaml/RFCs#60](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/60))
- **Tuple polymorphism scope** clarified by [Alistair](https://github.com/johnyob) in response to concerns about typechecker complexity, implicit layout reordering breaking FFI guarantees, and the diminished motivation for polymorphic `fst`/`snd` after omnidirectional typing improvements in [ocaml/RFCs](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs) ([ocaml/RFCs#57](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/57))
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Standard library modernization** continuing with practical string manipulation functions in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) ([ocaml/ocaml#14437](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14437), [ocaml/ocaml#14436](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14436), [ocaml/ocaml#14487](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14487)), runtime configuration improvements ([ocaml/ocaml#14367](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14367)), and active stdlib team evaluation process for new API proposals ([ocaml/ocaml#13101](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13101), [ocaml/ocaml#14393](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14393))
- **Platform support expansion** with FreeBSD profiling infrastructure in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) ([ocaml/ocaml#14486](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14486)), Power backend CFI support ([ocaml/ocaml#14482](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14482)), Windows version requirements clarification ([ocaml/ocaml#14484](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14484)), and cross-compilation toolchain discussions ([ocaml/ocaml#14478](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14478))
- **Build system integration** emerging as central design consideration with references to Buck2 and Bazel worker protocols informing the persistent compiler RFC design in [ocaml/RFCs](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs) ([ocaml/RFCs#60](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/60))
- **Determinism and reproducibility** improvements addressing compilation output consistency in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) ([ocaml/ocaml#14483](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14483)) and build system simplification ([ocaml/ocaml#14488](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14488)), with reproducibility concerns also raised in persistent compiler RFC discussions ([ocaml/RFCs#60](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/60))
- **Type system soundness** receiving attention with polymorphic pattern bug fixes in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) ([ocaml/ocaml#14480](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14480), [ocaml/ocaml#14479](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14479)) and shape occurrence handling ([ocaml/ocaml#14476](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14476)), while RFC discussions increasingly consider interaction with existing features and typechecker complexity ([ocaml/RFCs#57](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/57))
- **Performance versus complexity trade-offs** continuing to shape RFC discussions in [ocaml/RFCs](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs), with proposals requiring demonstration of concrete benefits sufficient to justify implementation and maintenance costs ([ocaml/RFCs#60](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/60), [ocaml/RFCs#57](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/57))
- **Modern C standards adoption** with C2y `_Countof` operator usage in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) ([ocaml/ocaml#14413](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14413)) and C++ compatibility improvements ([ocaml/ocaml#14490](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14490))
- **AI contribution policies** established with clear disclosure requirements and contributor responsibility guidelines in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) ([ocaml/ocaml#14052](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14052), [ocaml/ocaml#14383](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14383))

- **Experimental lock-free hash table**: [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic) continued development of lock-free hash table with incremental resizing in draft PR with 823 lines added across 8 files in [ocaml-multicore/picos#382](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos/issues/382)
- **API ergonomics discussion**: [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) raised discussion about adding Fmt convenience functions to Flow and Buf_write for easier formatted output in production code without requiring traceln or Log frameworks, which was closed in [ocaml-multicore/eio#829](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/829)
group_summaries.notable_discussions: - **Eio formatted output API**: Discussion about adding Fmt convenience functions to [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) for production code output formatting was opened and closed by [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) in [ocaml-multicore/eio#829](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/829)
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Platform-specific I/O handling**: Continued focus on MacOS-specific device I/O edge cases in [ocaml-multicore/eio#827](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/827)
- **Dependency maintenance**: Regular updates to shared dependencies like multicore-magic across the Picos package ecosystem in [ocaml-multicore/picos#386](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos/issues/386)
- **Experimental data structures**: Ongoing development of advanced concurrent data structures with lock-free hash table work in [ocaml-multicore/picos#382](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos/issues/382)

weekly_summary.brief_summary: Core stdlib enhancements merged, persistent compiler RFC proposed, oxcaml advanced Flambda2 and ARM64 work, tools saw code modernization
weekly_summary.new_features_summary: String split/replace, Gc.Tweak tuning, FreeBSD profiling, native expect tests, type navigation, ES6+ JS parser support
weekly_summary.new_features:
- __RUMINANT:core__ **String module enhancements** with `split_first`, `split_last`, `split_all`, and `rsplit_all` for splitting by separator in [ocaml/ocaml#14437](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14437) plus `replace_first`, `replace_last`, and `replace_all` for substring replacement in [ocaml/ocaml#14436](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14436) by [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli)

weekly_summary.group_overview:
- __RUMINANT:core__ The **standard library** received three merged functions including string split and replace operations by [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli) in [ocaml/ocaml#14437](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14437) and [ocaml/ocaml#14436](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14436), runtime GC tuning via `Gc.Tweak` in [ocaml/ocaml#14367](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14367), and FreeBSD profiling support in [ocaml/ocaml#14486](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14486)
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Type system reliability** received attention with soundness fixes for polymorphic patterns in [ocaml/ocaml#14480](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14480) and [ocaml/ocaml#14479](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14479), shape occurrence handling in [ocaml/ocaml#14476](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14476), and deterministic compilation restored in [ocaml/ocaml#14483](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14483)
- __RUMINANT:core__ In [ocaml/RFCs](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs), [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) proposed a **persistent compiler RFC** in [ocaml/RFCs#60](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/60) to reduce build times by keeping compiler instances running, generating discussion about protocol design and performance expectations
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ The **Flambda2 upstreaming** effort intensified with [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) creating 32 organizational issues covering match-in-match optimization in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5269](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5269), lifted constant handling in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5295](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5295), zero-alloc analysis in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5278](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5278), and reaper analysis in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5267](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5267)
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Seamm backend initiative** launched with 11 tracking issues defining a new egraph-based compiler backend including control flow language in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5257](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5257), Flambda 2 translation in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5258](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5258), and egraph core library in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5260](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5260)
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **ARM64 platform maturity** advancing through platform-independent JIT in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5186](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5186), wide vector translation to tuples in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5291](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5291), and binary emitter verification in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5198](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5198) by [Max Slater](https://github.com/TheNumbat)
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Package ecosystem integration** improved with [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) enabling coinstallation of oxcaml packages via opam-repomin plugin in [oxcaml/opam-repository#32](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/pull/32) and js_of_ocaml integration in [oxcaml/opam-repository#31](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/pull/31)
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ Activity concentrated in [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) with [Aaron Eline](https://github.com/aaronjeline) continuing **MacOS block device** support work in [ocaml-multicore/eio#827](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/827), while [ocaml-multicore/picos](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos) upgraded dependencies in [ocaml-multicore/picos#386](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos/issues/386)
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ The [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) merged 29 packages including new tools like opam-repomin in [ocaml/opam-repository#29274](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29274), csexp-query in [ocaml/opam-repository#29273](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29273), ortools in [ocaml/opam-repository#29159](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29159), and domainpc in [ocaml/opam-repository#29223](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29223)
